title: Andinocleome glandulosa
intro: Andinocleome glandulosa, also known as 'Balsam Apple', is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Balsam apple family (Cleomaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Andinocleome glandulosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal medicine to prepare infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are commonly used to treat skin conditions, fever, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Andinocleome glandulosa is classified within the family Bignonaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the genus Andinocleome, which is a part of the order Lamiales. The classification also includes family rank, with Bignonaceae being a family of flowering plants. At the order level, Lamiales, Andinocleome glandulosa is part of a diverse group of flowering plants. This classification highlights the plant's relationships within the broader plant kingdom.
benefits: Andinocleome glandulosa is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various health issues.
